message:
fix(transport): clean up channel waiters on drop and refresh stale wakers

Both ChannelSink and ChannelStream had resource lifecycle bugs:

ChannelSink:
- Dropping a sink that was waiting for capacity left a stale entry in the
  shared send_wakers list, leaking memory and causing spurious wakeups.
  Added a Drop impl that marks the waiter as inactive and removes it.
- Re-polling an already-queued waiter kept the original waker even if
  the task migrated to a different executor context. Now poll_ready
  updates the stored waker to the current one on every poll.
- New tests: waiter list is empty after dropping a pending sink; a
  re-polled sink uses the latest waker when capacity becomes available.

ChannelStream:
- Same stale-waiter leak as the sink side. Added a Drop impl that marks
  the waiter as inactive and removes it from the recv_wakers list.
- New test: waiter list is empty after dropping a pending stream.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
